title: HL7 Da Vinci PAS 2.2.1 Medical Prior-Authorization Review Binding

source_contract.trust_boundary: The caller supplies Claim, ClaimResponse, policy, pairwise-patient mapping, and reviewer evidence from authenticated server-owned systems of record. Agent, patient-facing, and request-body assertions are not trust sources.
required_relationships: ClaimResponse.request is the exact Claim/{id} reference
Claim and ClaimResponse patient references match before projection
Claim and ClaimResponse insurer references match before projection
Claim.item.sequence and ClaimResponse.item.itemSequence form the same unique set
Each response item has exactly one supported PAS review-action code

digested_server_projections.service_request_digest: Claim careTeam, supportingInfo, diagnosis, procedure, insurance, and item fields
digested_server_projections.decision_digest: FHIR processing outcome plus ordered response item sequences, PAS review-action codes, and exact adjudication digests

accepted_identity_evidence: fhir_reviewer_digest equals the exact reviewer-extension digest
accepted_authority_evidence: policy_digest equals the bound decision policy
claim_response_digest equals the bound server ClaimResponse
adverse_review.acceptance_boundary: Accepted means the relying party has already verified and accepted the evidence under its own trust policy. This reference module validates the binding and does not verify issuer signatures, licenses, registries, or revocation by itself.

prohibits: direct Patient references or patient identifiers
names, birth dates, and contact information
raw diagnosis, procedure, service, supportingInfo, or clinical-note fields
unknown or extension fields outside the portable allowlist
portable_output.privacy_claim: This is a deterministic minimization control, not a HIPAA, CMIA, security, or legal-compliance determination.

replay.verifier_input: The caller supplies its expected operation/CAID and durable consumed-CAID state. The pure verifier refuses a consumed CAID but does not persist consumption.

pharmacy_rail.note: Drug prior authorization and pharmacy-benefit workflows stay on the applicable NCPDP SCRIPT/ePA and related pharmacy standards. This PAS profile is only the medical prior-authorization rail.

regulatory_context.california_sb_1120: SB 1120 makes licensed-professional review of adverse medical-necessity decisions an auditable operational event; it does not mandate EMILIA, CAIDs, signatures, or cryptographic receipts.
regulatory_context.cms_wiser: CMS WISeR requires appropriately licensed human clinical review for non-affirmations, creating an auditable licensed-review event; it does not mandate EMILIA, CAIDs, signatures, or cryptographic receipts.

claim_boundaries: No production, deployment, availability, or EHR-integration claim.
No claim that a digest proves source authenticity or that a CAID proves authorization.
No legal, regulatory, clinical, medical-necessity, privacy-compliance, or payer-policy determination.
No claim of HL7, Da Vinci, CMS, California, or NCPDP endorsement.
